summary: Real full-integer TFLite INT8 accuracy vs PyTorch FP32/QDQ-proxy, TCN, 5 folds x seed 42, stride-1 full test set (N=full per-fold test windows, same protocol as compression pipeline).
method: ai_edge_litert TFLite interpreter running the actual exported INT8 model (experiments/esp32/scripts/export_tflite.py, full-integer PTQ, default 100 calibration windows, calib-seed=42) over every stride-1 test window for that fold (not a subsample). No ESP32-C3 hardware involved -- this measures accuracy only, not latency (see esp32_c3_metrics.json for on-device latency).
note_fold4_calibration_sensitivity: fold4 (default calib: 100 windows, seed=42) collapsed to 70.44% macro F1 (-20.90pp vs FP32). Re-exporting with calib-seed=123 and 400 windows improved it to 75.28% (-16.06pp) -- still a severe outlier relative to folds 0-3, indicating both (a) calibration-set quality materially affects full-integer PTQ accuracy, and (b) at least one checkpoint (fold4) is intrinsically more quantization-sensitive regardless of calibration, unlike the uniform near-zero degradation the PyTorch QDQ (weight-only) proxy predicts.
